seoTitle: Frankland River vs Fremantle — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Frankland River and Fremantle. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Frankland River vs Fremantle: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frankland River and Fremantle.
heading: Frankland River vs Fremantle — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frankland River, Western Australia and Fremantle, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frankland River has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Fremantle (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frankland River or Fremantle safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frankland River has a flood risk score of 50/100, while Fremantle scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frankland River scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Fremantle scores 37/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frankland River or Fremantle?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frankland River has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.28% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
